The primary objective of covered writing, for most investors, is increased income
through stock ownership. An ever-increasing number of private and institutional
investors a
rule_text: Own stock and write call options against it to increase income; this strategy outperforms outright stock ownership if the stock falls, remains flat, or rises slightly, but underperforms if the stock rises substantially.

Write (sell) call options against the owned stock

One final word of caution must be inserted. The investor should not attempt to
become “fancy” with the income-bearing portion of his assets. T-bills may appear to
be too “tame”
rule_text: Do not use higher-risk fixed-income securities (GNMAs, corporate bonds, convertible bonds, municipal bonds) or alternative investments (high-yield stocks, covered call writing) for the income-bearing portion of assets; use Treasury bills instead to avoid excessive risk.

rule_text: Select a futures quantity such that the dollar multiplier (futures contract value per point × quantity) creates a mini-index portfolio whose total capitalization matches the index value multiplied by the multiplier, enabling direct index value reading with decimal point adjustment.

Buy stock equal to: index value × multiplier (e.g., 170.25 × $10,000 = $1,702,500)
